gliders.name: Azuramean Owl
gliders.description: In the rocky grasslands of Azuram, the owls grew so big they started abducting human travelers. Eventually, the travelers got used to it and started using these animals as a means of transport.

gliders.name: Eksodean Dragoon
gliders.description: High above Skover’s floating fields, the local creatures had to adjust fast to life in the sky. That’s how the glider-drakes came to be small, friendly dragon pets with soft wings and an easy glide. They flit between the hovering cliffs like playful paper kites, happy to follow anyone who offers a warm hand and a little adventure.

gliders.name: Enripian Featherbeak
gliders.description: The two large feathers on the back of the Featherbeaks' wings help them stabilize their flight as they swoop down on their prey. They also play a role in courtship displays: the males with the most colorful feathers are the ones that attract the females.

gliders.name: Meropsian Wingfish
gliders.description: Wingfish are truly strange creatures: nature has endowed them with wings for swimming and fins for flying, which, contrary to what one might think, makes them very poor at both.

gliders.name: Pink Moth
gliders.description: Moths are fascinating creatures capable of releasing powders with various effects: medicinal, hallucinogenic, soporific, poisonous... You should always avoid touching a moth with your fingers.

gliders.name: Semeruian Seedbird
gliders.description: When Seedbirds are hungry, it’s said that they nibble on the tips of their roots and eventually fall to the ground after eating their wings. There, they grow back the following season.

sources.text: Not available in the current version.

gliders.name: Skoverial Raccoon
gliders.description: On the ethereal grounds that soared above Skover, many animal species had to quickly adapt to a newly aerial environment. In other words, raccoons can fly now.

sources.text: Mage starter glider (granted at character creation).

gliders.name: Skoverian Bat
gliders.description: The bats of Siagarta have developed the unfortunate habit of drinking their master's blood while they sleep.
